seed_problem: Place watchtowers on a forest graph to cover every cell within radius r, minimizing total cost; score = baseline_cost / achieved_cost.
mutation: composite: replace uniform watchtower cost + geometric forest with a heavy-tailed per-zone install cost on an irregular grid-plus-shortcut duct graph, turning the covering task into a weighted distance-r dominating set (facility location) where graph hop distance (not geometry) defines coverage, so LP-rounding and cost-effectiveness greedy diverge from plain coverage greedy.
title: Verdant Vault: Greenhouse Climate-Zone Coverage
strategies: trivial full install: put a regulator in every zone (always feasible, equals the baseline cost)
coverage greedy: repeatedly install the regulator that newly covers the most uncovered zones, ignoring cost
cost-effectiveness greedy: repeatedly install the regulator minimizing cost per newly covered zone, then prune redundant regulators
randomized multi-restart local search: perturb the greedy order across seeds and keep the cheapest feasible cover found
